steps.title: Identify the context clue for “soft”
steps.content: Alice immediately follows “soft” with a comparison: the snow sounds “just as if someone was kissing the window all over outside.” A kiss on a window is a quiet, tender, delicate action. The word “soft” in this context describes that gentle, light quality of sound—not texture, brightness, or interest.

steps.title: Match each answer choice to the context clue
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Gentle’: Directly captures the quiet, tender quality evoked by the kissing comparison and the word “nice.” ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Sensitive’: “Sensitive” describes a capacity to feel or react; it does not describe how a sound quality compares to a gentle kiss. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Shapeless’: Refers to lacking a definite form. Alice is describing how the snow sounds, not how it looks or what shape it has. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Bland’: Means dull or uninteresting. Alice calls the sound “nice,” showing she finds it pleasant—the opposite of bland. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
